title: Local AI Agents Masterclass (Gemma 4 + OpenCode + Odysseus tutorial)
summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates how to run two types of AI agents locally: Odysseus (a workspace agent) for task orchestration and OpenCode (a build agent) for code generation. The setup uses Gemma 4 via Ollama for local inference with Venice API as fallback, deployed in Docker for privacy-focused local execution.
writeup: The tutorial walks through running two distinct local AI agents: Odysseus, a workspace agent for task orchestration, and OpenCode, a build agent for code generation. Inference runs on Gemma 4 through Ollama, with the Venice API configured as a fallback, and the whole setup is deployed in Docker for privacy-focused local execution. The takeaway is a concrete multi-agent pattern that separates orchestration from code generation while keeping computation on local hardware.
